title: Walking Skeleton, Core Behavior, and Preserved Invariants · Evidence Recall
summary: A delayed review reconstructs walking skeleton, core behavior, and preserved invariants from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for cumulative working product with invariant evidence after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Deliver the smallest accessible end-to-end path through real boundaries with observable build, test, deploy, and recovery signals.
Implement one domain operation with explicit preconditions, transition, postconditions, failure taxonomy, and stakeholder-visible result.
Encode critical invariants at the closest layer and prove a deliberate violation is rejected without corrupting prior state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Walking Skeleton
steps.instruction:
Order the Walking Skeleton evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Walking Skeleton.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Walking Skeleton assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Walking Skeleton, keep Walking Skeleton connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Deliver the smallest accessible end-to-end path through real boundaries with observable build, test, deploy, and recovery signals.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Walking Skeleton has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Walking Skeleton, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Walking Skeleton precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Walking Skeleton, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Deliver the smallest accessible end-to-end path through real boundaries with observable build, test, deploy, and recovery signals.

For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Walking Skeleton, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A static mock connected to placeholder data is an operational walking skeleton.

stimulus.title: Refactor Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Passing type checks proves a refactor preserved behavior.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change. Artifact under review: cumulative working product with invariant evidence.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Refactor Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Refactor Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Refactor Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
